# Marp Player — Complete Documentation

> Free online Markdown-to-Slides presentation player powered by Marp Core.
> URL: https://marp-play.techbuzzz.me

## Overview

Marp Player is a professional web application that converts Marp-flavored Markdown into presentation slides. It provides real-time preview, PDF export, fullscreen mode, speaker notes, and multiple navigation methods. The tool is free, open-source, and runs entirely in the browser with server-side rendering.

## Architecture

### Rendering Pipeline
1. User inputs Marp Markdown in the editor panel
2. After 500ms debounce, markdown is sent to `/api/render-marp`
3. Server renders via `@marp-team/marp-core` with `html: true, script: false`
4. Output: SVG-wrapped slides (`<div class="marpit"><svg data-marpit-svg>...</svg></div>`)
5. Each SVG contains a `<foreignObject>` with a `<section>` element
6. Global CSS is extracted and injected via `<style>` tag
7. Speaker notes extracted from `<aside class="notes">` and HTML comments

### API Routes
- `POST /api/render-marp` — Renders Marp markdown, returns `{ slides: [{html, notes}], css }`
- `POST /api/export-pdf` — Renders all slides to HTML, converts to PDF via Playwright-based API
- `GET /api/markdown?file=<name>` — Loads example markdown files from `public/examples/`

## Features in Detail

### 1. Marp Syntax Support
Full compatibility with @marp-team/marp-core:
- YAML frontmatter (marp, theme, paginate, footer, header, size, style)
- Three built-in themes: default, gaia, uncover
- Custom CSS via `style:` frontmatter block
- Scoped directives: `<!-- _class: -->`, `<!-- _backgroundColor: -->`, `<!-- _paginate: -->`
- Background images: `![bg](url)`, `![bg left](url)`, `![bg right:40%](url)`
- Image sizing: `![width:300px](url)`, `![height:200px](url)`
- Math: inline `$E=mc^2$` and block `$$\int f(x)dx$$`
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting
- HTML content (tables, divs, custom classes)
- Presenter notes via `<!-- notes -->` or `<aside class="notes">`

### 2. Navigation Methods
- **Keyboard**: Arrow keys (←→), Space (next), Home (first), End (last), F (fullscreen), Escape (exit)
- **Click-to-advance**: Toggle switch in toolbar. Click anywhere on slide to advance. Loops back to first slide.
- **Auto-play**: Play/Pause button with interval selector (1s, 2s, 3s, 4s, 5s, 10s). Stops at last slide.
- **Navigator buttons**: First, Previous, Next, Last buttons with slide counter

### 3. PDF Export
- Server-side rendering: Marp → HTML → PDF
- Landscape 16:9 format (1280×720px per page)
- One slide per page with CSS page breaks
- All custom CSS styles preserved
- Background graphics printed
- Async generation with loading indicator

### 4. Fullscreen Mode
- Press F or click Fullscreen button
- All controls available in fullscreen bar
- Click-to-advance works in fullscreen
- Escape exits properly (synced with browser fullscreenchange event)
- Auto-play controls in fullscreen

### 5. Editor Panel
- Textarea with monospace font for markdown editing
- Load files: .md, .markdown, .txt (direct), .json (Marp Player format)
- Save as JSON (includes markdown + timestamp)
- Example button loads comprehensive demo presentation
- Collapsible on mobile devices

### 6. Speaker Notes
- Extracted from HTML comments and `<aside>` tags
- Displayed in collapsible panel below slide viewer
- Preserved formatting with pre-wrap

### 7. Themes
- App themes: Modern, Minimal, Dark, Light (UI styling)
- Marp themes: default, gaia, uncover (slide content styling)
- Custom CSS via frontmatter overrides theme styles

### 8. Responsive Design
- Mobile-first layout with Tailwind CSS
- Stacked panels on small screens (editor top, viewer bottom)
- Side-by-side on desktop (md+ breakpoint)
- Compact navigation on mobile (no First/Last buttons)
- Collapsible editor panel
- Touch-friendly buttons

## Custom CSS Examples

### Grid Layout
```html
<div class="grid-2">
  <div>Column 1</div>
  <div>Column 2</div>
</div>
```

CSS in frontmatter:
```css
.grid-2 { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 24px; }
.grid-3 { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr; gap: 20px; }
```

### Cards
```css
.card {
  background: white;
  border: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 24px;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
}
```

### Styled Sections
```markdown
<!-- _class: lead -->
# Title Slide

<!-- _class: dark -->
## Dark Background Slide
```

## Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 14 (App Router, TypeScript)
- **Rendering**: @marp-team/marp-core (server-side dynamic import)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui components
- **PDF Export**: Abacus HTML2PDF API (Playwright-based)
- **Fonts**: DM Sans, Plus Jakarta Sans, JetBrains Mono
- **Deployment**: Docker (multi-stage build) or Abacus AI hosting

## Keyboard Shortcuts Reference
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| → / Space | Next slide |
| ← | Previous slide |
| Home | First slide |
| End | Last slide |
| F | Toggle fullscreen |
| Escape | Exit fullscreen / Show editor |

## API for AI Agents & Automation (MCP + REST)

Marp Player exposes **two** integration paths:

1. **Native MCP server** at `POST /api/v1/mcp` — JSON-RPC 2.0 over the MCP
   Streamable HTTP transport. Point Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline or any other
   MCP client at this URL.
2. **REST API** with OpenAPI 3.1 spec at `/api/v1/openapi` — intended for
   OpenAI GPTs, LangChain, LlamaIndex and other frameworks that consume plain
   tool definitions.

### Authentication (applies to both paths)
- Generate a Bearer token in the MCP modal of the Marp Player UI
  (`POST /api/v1/keys` behind the scenes — token shown only once, hashed at rest).
- Or set `MARP_API_KEYS` (comma-separated list) env var on the server.
- Pass it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
- If neither is configured, the API runs in open mode (dev convenience).

### MCP endpoint — POST /api/v1/mcp

Accepts a single JSON-RPC 2.0 message or a batch. Supported methods:

| Method | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `initialize` | Handshake; returns server info + capabilities |
| `ping` | Liveness check |
| `tools/list` | Enumerate tools |
| `tools/call` | Invoke a tool |
| `notifications/initialized` | Client acknowledged ready (no response) |

MCP tools exposed:

- `create_share_link({ markdown, title?, expiresInHours? })` → `{ id, playUrl, embedUrl, slideCount, expiresAt }`
- `render_pdf({ markdown, filename? })` → base64 `resource` content (`application/pdf`)
- `get_presentation({ id })` → `{ id, title, markdown, slideCount, createdAt, expiresAt }`
- `delete_presentation({ id })` → confirmation

Minimal Claude Desktop config:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "marp-player": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://marp-play.techbuzzz.me/api/v1/mcp",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" }
    }
  }
}
```

Sample raw handshake (no client library):
```bash
curl -X POST https://marp-play.techbuzzz.me/api/v1/mcp \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": 1,
    "method": "initialize",
    "params": {
      "protocolVersion": "2025-06-18",
      "capabilities": {},
      "clientInfo": { "name": "demo", "version": "1.0.0" }
    }
  }'
```

### REST endpoints

#### POST /api/v1/render-pdf
Convert Marp Markdown to a high-quality 16:9 landscape PDF.

Request:
```json
{
  "markdown": "---\nmarp: true\ntheme: default\npaginate: true\n---\n\n# Title\n\nContent\n\n---\n\n## Slide 2\n\nMore content",
  "filename": "my-presentation.pdf"
}
```
Response: Binary PDF file (application/pdf)

#### POST /api/v1/play
Store Marp Markdown and receive a shareable interactive presentation URL.

Request:
```json
{
  "markdown": "---\nmarp: true\n---\n\n# Hello\n\n---\n\n## World",
  "title": "My Presentation",
  "expiresInHours": 72  // optional, default: 720 (30 days)
}
```
Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "id": "clx1234abc",
  "playUrl": "https://marp-play.techbuzzz.me/play/clx1234abc",
  "embedUrl": "https://marp-play.techbuzzz.me/play/clx1234abc?embed=true",
  "title": "My Presentation",
  "slideCount": 2,
  "createdAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "expiresAt": "2025-01-04T00:00:00.000Z"
}
```

#### GET /api/v1/presentations/{id}
Retrieve stored presentation markdown and metadata.

#### DELETE /api/v1/presentations/{id}
Permanently delete a stored presentation.

#### GET /api/v1/openapi
Full OpenAPI 3.1 specification for registering these endpoints as tools.

#### GET /api/v1
API index with all endpoint URLs.

### Usage with AI Agents

Pick the right path for the platform you are wiring up:

| Platform | Integration | URL |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Claude Desktop / Cursor / Cline | Native MCP (Streamable HTTP) | `/api/v1/mcp` |
| OpenAI GPTs (Actions) | OpenAPI 3.1 spec | `/api/v1/openapi` |
| LangChain / LlamaIndex | OpenAPI 3.1 spec | `/api/v1/openapi` |
| Custom / CLI | Raw REST | `/api/v1/*` |

Example use cases:
- "Create a presentation from these meeting notes and give me the link"
- "Export this Marp markdown as PDF"
- "Generate a 10-slide deck about X and share it"

## Getting Started
1. Open Marp Player in your browser
2. Paste Marp Markdown in the editor (left panel)
3. Or click "Example" to load the demo presentation
4. Use toolbar controls or keyboard to navigate
5. Click PDF button to export
6. Press F for fullscreen presentation

## Self-Hosting
```bash
git clone https://github.com/techbuzzz/marp-play.git
cd marp-play/nextjs_space
yarn install
yarn dev
```

Or with Docker:
```bash
docker-compose up
```
